topic: NP pre-1990 linear drift — verify PATCH-A (ISS-1912 NP.xy model-reconstructed clarification)
change_summary: PATCH-A (EXP-366/ISS-1912) inserted ~130-char parenthetical into detail_verdict_text clarifying that NOAA NGDC NP.xy is a model-reconstructed product (gufm1 pre-1900 + IGRF modern era), not a raw measurement file, with IGRF-14 as the current IGRF generation.
our_argument_summary: WIN-006 (verdict: Std Model Explains, NP pre-1990 linear drift) is non-discriminating: both standard core-mantle convection and the dome's 'aetheric vortex' can fit the same NOAA pole-position curve, and the dome model provides no derivation from dome geometry that produces a specific testable drift rate. Post-hoc test also fails because the confirming dataset (NP.xy) was publicly available before the dome monitor commit, and PATCH-A now adds a parenthetical making clear that NP.xy is itself a model-reconstructed product (gufm1 + IGRF) — the trivial-transcription post-hoc objection applies regardless of whether the dataset is raw or reconstructed.

verification_evidence.note: Sentence remains a single readable clause; the parenthetical reads naturally and does not break the surrounding argument. The 134-char delta matches the patch's predicted '~135 character' length addition.

iss_1900_collateral_verification.description: WIN-006.c3 hole [0] (major) reported a verbatim duplication of the '0.0466 deg/yr ... cannot be reproduced from the NOAA NP.xy dataset by any standard metric' sentence in detail_verdict_text. ISS-1900 was self-applied separately by the decider.
iss_1900_collateral_verification.grep_result: Exactly one occurrence of '0.0466 deg/yr' in current detail_verdict_text — duplication has been removed cleanly.

iss_1921_collateral_verification.description: WIN-006.c3 hole [2] (minor) reported that 'Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well' in paragraph 1 of detail_verdict_text conceded too much. ISS-1921 was self-applied separately by the decider.
iss_1921_collateral_verification.grep_result: Phrase 'Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well' is absent from detail_verdict_text — paragraph 1 has been rewritten to say the dome's claimed rate 'dissolves on contact with data'.
iss_1921_collateral_verification.outcome: PASS in detail_verdict_text — BUT the same phrase still appears verbatim in tldr_verdict (see new hole below).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Monitor fetches NOAA pole positions (NP.xy from NGDC) and compares observed pre-1990 drift to a hardcoded target rate. No fresh code inspection needed for this verification — PATCH-A modifies only review prose, not the dome's automation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not re-checked this run — prior cycle established the dome compares a hardcoded target against the NP.xy fetch.
data_sources_used: NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position file

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Inherited from c3 review — NP.xy is fetched via NOAA, then compared against hardcoded target. PATCH-A does not change the live_fetch nature; if anything, it sharpens our argument that the 'fetched' dataset is itself a reconstruction.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Linear pole drift is fully explained by core-mantle convection (gufm1 / IGRF historical reconstructions). The dome model relabels the cause as 'aetheric vortex dynamics' without producing a distinct rate or mechanism.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: NP.xy historical pole-position files have been publicly available for decades. The patched detail_verdict_text now correctly notes NP.xy is itself a gufm1+IGRF reconstruction — this strengthens (not weakens) the post-hoc argument because the trivial-transcription test applies to indexed published reconstructions just as it does to raw data files.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc_radius / firmament_height / aetheric parameters to the claimed 0.0466 deg/yr rate exists on the dome site; the dome's claimed rate is asserted rather than derived. PATCH-A is consistent with this finding.

holes_found.description: tldr_verdict still reads 'Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well. The dome's aetheric vortex label adds nothing — no distinct prediction, no mechanism, no derivation.' The leading sentence is verbatim what ISS-1921 patched OUT of detail_verdict_text on the grounds that it concedes too much given the post-hoc paragraph shows the dome's 0.0466 deg/yr rate cannot be reproduced from NP.xy. The TLDR is now out of sync with the detail: detail says the dome's match 'dissolves on contact with data', TLDR says 'fit equally well'. Per the curmudgeon TLDR rule, a TLDR that contradicts the section it summarizes is a finding; this one is borderline (it remains a valid simplification of the high-level 'non-discriminating' point) but it overstates how well the dome model actually fits and a defender could quote the TLDR back at us as a concession.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite tldr_verdict first sentence to track the patched detail_verdict_text. Proposed: 'Both models can be drawn through the same NOAA points, but the dome's specific claimed rate (0.0466 deg/yr) cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric. The aetheric vortex label adds nothing — no distinct prediction, no mechanism, no derivation.' This is roughly 60 chars longer than the current 151-char TLDR; trim 'distinct prediction, no mechanism, no derivation' to 'no mechanism, no derivation' if length matters.
holes_found.proposed_issue_text: WIN-006 tldr_verdict still contains the soft phrasing 'Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well' that ISS-1921 patched out of detail_verdict_text. TLDR is now out of sync with the sharpened detail (0.0466 deg/yr unreproducible from NP.xy). Apply the same rewrite to tldr_verdict.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: WIN-006.c3 hole [3] (minor) — verdict-category boundary between WIN-006 (Std Model Explains) and WIN-007/WIN-022 (both Misleading) is still soft. ISS-1913 currently assigned-analyst (not in scope for this verification batch). Not re-flagged in current holes_found; tracking via unresolved_prior_cycle so the decider's M3 carry-over enforcement sees it.

kernel_of_truth.description: The patch is well-conceived and well-applied. The clarification that NP.xy is gufm1+IGRF reconstructed (data/wins.json:006.detail_verdict_text) genuinely preempts a defender pivot — 'oh, NP.xy is just raw NOAA measurements, you're misreading the post-hoc test' — that a careful reader might otherwise raise. The wording 'a model-reconstructed product blending gufm1 for the pre-1900 era and the IGRF series for the modern era' is accurate to how NOAA NGDC actually documents this product, and 'with IGRF-14 as the current IGRF generation' correctly names the May-2025-issued current IGRF model.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: It is a verification of an internal review patch, not a defense of the dome model. The patch strengthens our post-hoc argument (per the EXP-366 PATCH-A rationale: trivial-transcription test applies regardless of raw-vs-reconstructed status). The verdict 'Std Model Explains' remains correct because the dome offers no derivation that produces a different drift rate from core-mantle convection (see data/wins.json:006.code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false, validated this cycle).

deeper_analysis: PATCH-A is exactly the kind of low-cost preemptive clarification this review benefits from. The dome site (john09289.github.io/predictions) has not historically argued that NP.xy is raw vs reconstructed, so this is a hypothetical defender pivot rather than a documented one — but the cost of pre-empting it is minimal (one parenthetical, ~130 chars) and the value if/when raised is high (it would be tedious to explain mid-debate). The collateral check that ISS-1900 (verbatim duplication) and ISS-1921 (soft 'fit equally well' phrasing in detail) both landed cleanly is also positive — three c3 holes resolved in one batch of self-applies, with only one new minor finding (TLDR sync) emerging. Batch context: this is item 1 of 2 in the q346/q347 verification batch.

citations_verified: NOAA NGDC NP.xy file documented as gufm1+IGRF composite — public documentation
IGRF-14 confirmed as the current IGRF generation as of 2026
citations_unchecked: dome author's pre-registration claim — not re-checked this run; covered by earlier cycles

summary_for_decider: PATCH-A landed cleanly: detail_verdict_text now contains the gufm1+IGRF clarification exactly once and the old short citation form is gone. ISS-1900 (dup sentence) and ISS-1921 (soft phrasing in detail) also verified clean as collateral checks. One new MODERATE finding: tldr_verdict still contains the verbatim 'Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well' phrase that ISS-1921 removed from detail_verdict_text — TLDR is now out of sync with the sharpened detail. Suggest opening a minor-or-moderate issue to apply the same rewrite to tldr_verdict. Carry-over: ISS-1913 (verdict-category boundary with WIN-007/022) is still pending analyst; not re-flagged here, listed in unresolved_prior_cycle for decider M3 visibility.
